meta.description: The definitive East Java adventure in 5 days — Mount Bromo sunrise, Rainbow Village, Kajoetangan Dutch heritage, Coban Rondo waterfall, apple orchard, Museum Angkut, and Surabaya. Private tour from RM1688. Book with Dhesu Travel.
meta.keywords: Bromo Malang tour 5 days, Rainbow Village Malang, Kajoetangan heritage, Surabaya tour Malaysia, East Java holiday package, Mount Bromo sunrise, Indonesia 5 day tour

hero.title: 5 Days Bromo
hero.intro: East Java's most comprehensive tour: a legendary Mount Bromo sunrise by 4WD jeep, colourful Rainbow Village, Dutch colonial Kajoetangan Heritage, stunning waterfalls, apple orchards, Asia's largest transport museum, and Surabaya's historic landmarks — all at your own pace in a private tour.
hero.heroImageAlt: Mount Bromo active volcano at sunrise with Sea of Sand and Malang highland backdrop

includes: Hotel accommodation (4 nights: 1 night Bromo, 2 nights Malang, 1 night Surabaya)
All meals as specified (Day 1: Lunch & Dinner; Days 2–4: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner; Day 5: Breakfast)
4WD jeep tour to Mount Bromo viewpoint and crater
excludes: Optional horse riding at Bromo crater (own expense)

overview.heading: East Java's Greatest Hits — Five Full Days of Volcano, Culture & City
body: This 5-day tour is the most complete way to experience East Java. It combines the world-famous Bromo sunrise with the full breadth of Malang and Surabaya's highlights — from Dutch colonial heritage streets and a vibrant rainbow-painted village to a hidden waterfall, working apple orchard, and Asia's most extraordinary transport museum. The extra day over our 4-day itinerary allows a more relaxed pace, deeper Malang exploration, and additional Surabaya sightseeing.

All transport, meals, entrance fees, and your dedicated English-speaking guide are included from airport arrival to departure. Group discounts make this exceptional value for families and friend groups — five adults pay just RM1,488 per person, nine or more pay RM1,288. This is the East Java tour that covers everything, leaving nothing on the 'next time' list.

items.title: Mount Bromo Sunrise by 4WD Jeep
items.body: A 2:00 AM wake-up leads to a jeep convoy across the dark Sea of Sand to the Penanjakan or Mentigen viewpoint. As dawn breaks, Bromo's volcanic cone emerges from the caldera in a blaze of orange and gold — one of the most breathtaking natural spectacles in Southeast Asia.
items.imageAlt: Mount Bromo sunrise viewed from Penanjakan viewpoint across the Sea of Sand

items.title: Rainbow Village & Kajoetangan Heritage
items.body: Malang's Kampung Warna-Warni Jodipan is a former slum transformed into a riot of colour — every house, wall, and staircase painted in brilliant hues, making it one of Java's most photographed neighbourhoods. Nearby Kajoetangan Heritage district preserves Malang's Dutch colonial-era architecture in a beautifully restored heritage walk.
items.imageAlt: Colourful Rainbow Village Kampung Warna-Warni painted houses in Malang

items.title: Museum Angkut — Asia's Largest Transport Museum
items.body: Over 450 vehicles spanning every era and continent — vintage Dutch trams, 1950s American Cadillacs, classic Indonesian becak, and a flight simulator — housed across 13 brilliantly themed zones in Batu. Genuinely one of Indonesia's most impressive and entertaining museums.
items.imageAlt: Vintage car collection and themed zones at Museum Angkut Batu

items.title: Coban Rondo Waterfall & Apple Orchard
items.body: Hike through cool highland forest to reach the 60-metre Coban Rondo waterfall, then head to Kusuma Agro Apple Garden to pick your own apples straight from the tree. Batu's highland climate is ideal for apple cultivation — a uniquely East Java agricultural experience.
items.imageAlt: Coban Rondo waterfall 60m cascade in East Java highland forest

days.title: Arrive Surabaya > Tanggulangin Leather Industry > Bromo
days.description: Your guide greets you at Juanda International Airport, Surabaya. Head east with a stop at Tanggulangin Leather Industry — a large craft centre in Sidoarjo where artisans hand-stitch bags, wallets, briefcases, and shoes. Browse the showroom for genuine leather goods at factory prices. Continue into the volcanic highlands of East Java as the landscape transitions from low-lying plains to the cool Tengger massif. Arrive at Bromo by early evening for dinner and rest — set your alarm for 2:00 AM.
suggestedActivities: Browse leather goods at Tanggulangin craft industry
Photograph East Java's changing highland landscape
Early night to prepare for 2:00 AM wake-up

days.title: Mount Bromo Sunrise 4WD Tour > Batu City > Museum Angkut > Malang
days.description: Rise at 2:00 AM and board your private 4WD jeep for the drive across the Sea of Sand to the Penanjakan or Mentigen viewpoint. Arrive before dawn and watch as the sky transforms — first violet, then pink, then blazing orange and gold as sunrise illuminates Mount Bromo's smoking cone, the vast Tengger Caldera, and the towering outline of Mount Semeru behind. After the spectacle, descend to the Sea of Sand and walk (or take an optional horse ride) to Mount Bromo's active crater rim. Return to the hotel for a well-earned breakfast, then transfer to the cool highland city of Batu — 'The City of Apples'. Spend the afternoon at Museum Angkut, Asia's largest transport museum with 450+ vehicles and 13 themed zones, including a flight simulator. Continue to Malang for dinner and overnight.
suggestedActivities: Walk to Mount Bromo crater rim
Explore Batu city centre and apple stalls

days.title: Coban Rondo Waterfall > Apple Garden > Rainbow Village > Kajoetangan Heritage
days.description: After breakfast in Malang, drive into the cool forested hills to Coban Rondo — East Java's most beloved waterfall, a 60-metre cascade tumbling down the volcanic slopes of Mount Panderman. Walk the highland forest trail to the falls and enjoy the natural spray. Head to Kusuma Agro Apple Garden, a working orchard where staff guide you through apple varieties, picking techniques, and fruit selection — a hands-on agricultural experience unique to Batu's cool highland climate. After lunch, explore two of Malang's most vibrant neighbourhoods: Kampung Warna-Warni Jodipan, the famous Rainbow Village where every surface is painted in brilliant colours, and the adjacent Kajoetangan Heritage district — a beautifully preserved network of Dutch colonial-era streets with restored shophouses, heritage cafes, and old European architecture. Dinner and overnight in Malang.
suggestedActivities: Apple picking at Kusuma Agro orchard
Walking tour of Kajoetangan Heritage district

days.title: Malang > Surabaya City Tour > Historical Landmarks
days.description: After breakfast and hotel checkout, stop at Toko Oleh-Oleh Brawijaya Istana for a wide selection of East Java souvenirs — local snacks, batik, and Malang speciality foods. Transfer to Surabaya, Indonesia's second city and the 'City of Heroes'. Explore Surabaya's atmospheric old trading district: the historic Red Bridge (Jembatan Merah) over the Kalimas River, once the commercial heartbeat of colonial Surabaya; Jembatan Merah Plaza shopping area; Kembang Jepun Street, the old Chinese-Dutch commercial quarter; and the Batik Wholesale Market for discounted batik cloth. Visit the Submarine Monument — the actual decommissioned submarine KRI Pasopati 410, now a museum you can walk through. Dinner and overnight in Surabaya.
suggestedActivities: Walk the Red Bridge and old Surabaya waterfront
Shop at Kembang Jepun batik wholesale street
Tour inside KRI Pasopati submarine museum

days.title: Free Exploration > Departure
days.description: After a leisurely hotel breakfast, enjoy free time in Surabaya before your departure flight. Use the morning to explore the historic Arab Quarter around Masjid Ampel — one of Java's oldest and most revered mosques, surrounded by shops selling dates, prayer items, and halal street food. Alternatively, visit Tunjungan Plaza or Galaxy Mall for last-minute shopping. Your guide transfers you to Juanda International Airport for your departure flight back to Malaysia.
suggestedActivities: Visit Masjid Ampel and the Arab Quarter

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Hotel
hotelOptions.subheading: All hotels on twin-sharing basis. Bromo accommodation is at altitude — temperatures can reach near freezing before dawn.

whyBook.body: Dhesu Travel has been organising private East Java tours for Malaysian families and groups since 1988. Our local partners in Surabaya, Malang, and Bromo ensure the jeep is ready at 2 AM, the viewpoint position is secured, and every meal stop is a genuine local experience.

sections.heading: 5-Day Bromo & Malang Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body: The 5-day East Java tour is the definitive choice for Malaysian travellers who want to experience Mount Bromo without rushing. Fly from Kuala Lumpur to Surabaya (Juanda) on AirAsia, Malaysia Airlines, or Batik Air — no visa required for Malaysian passport holders. Over five full days, this private tour covers the Bromo sunrise, the cultural charm of Malang (Rainbow Village, Kajoetangan Heritage, Museum Angkut), highland apple orchards, Coban Rondo waterfall, and Surabaya's historical landmarks — all at an unhurried pace starting from RM1,688 per person twin-sharing.

sections.heading: Malang — East Java's Coolest City
sections.body: Malang sits at 440 metres above sea level, giving it a pleasantly cool climate year-round. The city is known for its Dutch colonial heritage (particularly in the Kajoetangan district), its creative arts scene, its proximity to the Bromo volcanic complex, and its vibrant café culture. Rainbow Village — transformed from a dilapidated riverside settlement into a colourful tourist attraction by local students in 2016 — has become one of Java's most visited photography spots. The surrounding highland area of Batu produces apples, strawberries, and vegetables unusual for a tropical country, giving East Java an agricultural dimension unlike anywhere else in Indonesia.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit Indonesia?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders can enter Indonesia visa-free for up to 30 days. Your passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your travel dates.

items.question: What time is the Mount Bromo sunrise and how cold does it get?
items.answer: Your guide wakes you at approximately 2:00 AM for the jeep departure. Sunrise occurs between 5:00 and 5:30 AM. Temperatures at the Penanjakan viewpoint can drop to 5°C or lower — bring a warm jacket, gloves, and a hat. The jeep provides some shelter during the drive but the viewpoint is open and very cold before sunrise.

items.question: Is Rainbow Village still popular or has it become too commercial?
items.answer: Kampung Warna-Warni Jodipan remains vibrant and photogenic, though it is a genuine neighbourhood where real families live. Early morning visits (which fit naturally into the Day 3 itinerary) offer a quieter experience before tourist coaches arrive. Your guide knows the best angles and quieter lanes.

items.question: How does this 5-day tour differ from the 4-day Bromo Sunrise Adventure?
items.answer: The 5-day tour adds Rainbow Village, Kajoetangan Heritage district, an extra night in Malang for a more relaxed pace, more Surabaya exploration including Jembatan Merah and the batik market, and additional souvenir shopping time. If you have 5 days, the extended itinerary gives a much richer Malang and Surabaya experience.

items.question: Are there suitable activities for young children?
items.answer: Yes — Museum Angkut, Rainbow Village, and the apple orchard are excellent for children. The Bromo sunrise involves a 2:00 AM start and cool temperatures, so we recommend it for children aged 8 and above. Your guide can adjust the pace on any day to suit your family.
